title: artocarpus nitidus subsp. griffithii
intro: Artocarpus nitidus subsp. griffithii, also known as the Kacip fatimah or black nut tree, is a species of plant that belongs to the Moraceae family. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Artocarpus nitidus subsp. griffithii include its ability to alleviate menstrual cramps, improve fertility, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a tea or infused in oil to extract its medicinal properties.
